summary_en: ROE 6.0% (FY3/26); PBR 0.42x. Target: PBR 1.0x+ via ROE improvement. Initiatives: sister-company collaboration on high-value products & joint procurement cost reduction; stable dividend growth aligned with earnings recovery; internal reserve buildup for capex & M&A. Medium-term horizon.
title_en: Update on Management Initiatives Oriented Toward Cost of Capital and Stock Price Awareness
translation_en_brief: Nippon Shindo released an update to its capital-efficiency and shareholder-value framework originally published May 2025. The company's FY3/26 ROE declined to 6.0% (from 8.4% the prior year) due to hedging losses on copper derivatives, despite higher copper spot prices and increased casting-rod sales volumes. PBR remains depressed at 0.42x, attributed to market skepticism about earnings sustainability, capital efficiency, and insufficiently accumulated retained earnings. The company targets PBR above 1.0x via three pillars: (1) revenue strength through deeper collaboration with sister firms Sannetsu Metals and Mitani Shindo on high-value products and joint procurement; (2) balanced growth investment and shareholder return, with stable dividend growth contingent on profitability recovery and balance-sheet strengthening; (3) internal reserve buildup to fund future capex and M&A. The horizon is medium-term, without specific numerical targets for ROE or payout ratio disclosed in this announcement.
